summary: Ensure minimum contrast 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for bold headlines ≥18px; white on #92278F, #262262, #01A47E passes; no silver or periwinkle on white.
All images require alt text; ISI must be live text, never image-based, ensuring communication with images off for indication and safety.
Accessibility: footnotes ≥10px live text; body text ≥12px; tap targets ≥44px; logical heading order; exactly one H1 per email.
Underline all links for accessibility; avoid color-only cues; apply semantic table markup with role="presentation" for non-tabular tables.
Each content section is a self-contained 600px table with its own background, top/bottom padding, optional wave edge, no outer margins, stacking flush (§2.2).
Sections must set theme backgrounds at top and bottom edges to ensure matching or wave transitions; first section meets header on white/theme, last ends matching locked footer wave.
Footnote symbols *, †, ‡, § run continuously across entire email, numbering references continuously through all sections including locked End Matter per template.
Max 1 Wave graphic & 1 key-strategic-question callout per email; 1 primary CTA per section; max ~3 CTAs total per email.
No new fonts, colors, button shapes, or icon styles allowed; new icons must match existing line weight/style; no logo lockup or ISI/legal copy reproduction outside locked components.
Final assembly QA confirms body size 12px/1.4 line-height, audience matches header/footer (§4.2), all §1.8 content sections, theme consistency, no horizontal scroll at both breakpoints.
Use primary colors Purple (30%), Dark Blue (30%), White (20%) for 'creative reliability'; accents Silver (15%), Dark Gray (≤15%), Green (5%); prefer Purple slightly in branded pieces.
Text colors: Titles Purple #92278F; Headlines/subheads Dark Blue #262262; Body/ISI black #000000 (corrected guide typo).
Never use Purple/Green for body copy; Green only for CTA fills/accents; Dark Gray for charts/secondary UI; never Silver for text.
Title and first email line must be purple; no other text in the email may use purple.
Highlight on white body section (not headings) must be black and bold; blue and purple disallowed in body paragraphs.
Each sentence, including headings and body, must use only one color; no mixing of two colors within the same sentence.
For Flip The Script campaign pieces, use Periwinkle, Purple, Green evenly at 33% each as accents supplementing branded palette.
Scene-color pairings fixed: Periwinkle→Office, Purple→Cafe, Green→Soccer; campaign colors locked to their respective scenes only.
Callout opacities over white: Periwinkle 80%, Purple 60%, Green 40%; white banner over imagery at 80%.
Primary background gradient: Dark Blue #262262 to Purple #92278F at 45°; light background gradient: Silver #CFDCE3 to White #FFFFFF, angle flexible.
Apply Dark Blue → Purple gradient sparingly to headlines and callouts, e.g., two-tone headline with back half shifting to Purple.
Use gradients only as background images or CSS with #92278F/#262262 fallback in email; never gradient body text, split headlines navy + purple instead.
Apply one theme per email frame from White, Silver/light, Purple #92278F, Navy #262262, Green #01A47E with matching text and button styles; do not theme sections independently.
Use white text on Purple/Navy/Green backgrounds; on White/Silver use navy headlines, black body, purple accents; links white underlined on dark, purple/navy underlined on light panels.

DTP email layout alternates backgrounds: purple hero → white content (efficacy+side-effects) → white/light photo (story/lifestyle) → green #01A47E accent (affordability/icons) → purple campaign image → locked End Matter.
Sections have no margin between edges; transitions use background color changes or full-bleed hero images, never dividers or rules.
Green panel (#01A47E) appears once per email, used exclusively for either affordability block or symptom-icon trio, never stacked together.
CTA button uses green fill (#01A47E) with white label on light backgrounds (White, Silver); white fill with green label on dark backgrounds (Dark Gray, Purple, Dark Blue, Green, Teal, Periwinkle).
In CTA section, on light backgrounds (White, Silver) heading is #262262 and body #000000; on dark backgrounds (Dark Gray, Purple, Dark Blue, Green, Teal, Periwinkle) both are #FFFFFF.

CTA imagery locked to background color: Email_Primary_2_column.png with Purple #92278F, Email_Primary_column_golf.png with Teal #0F99BC, Cake_lockup.png with Periwinkle #7B7BED; always dark background with white text/button, green label.
If content section above CTA is populated, CTA shows text+button only on approved background; on light backgrounds (e.g. Silver), button is green-filled with white label.
Use Teal #0F99BC background only in CTA sections with LPGA golf image (Email_Primary_column_golf.png); omit both if no LPGA content in email.
Desktop email width 600px fixed table; mobile width 375px fluid/hybrid layout collapsing below 480px; both versions for each component; SFMC serves automatically.
Component reference heights from library (Primary 1-Col 600×1138/375×788; Primary 2-Col 600×1088/375×1538; Secondary 600×458 desktop) are proportions, not fixed limits.
Spacing values must be multiples of 8px; adjust layouts only in 8px increments, no odd or one-off spacing values allowed.

Section spacing: container side padding 24px desktop/16px mobile (L&R), vertical padding 32px desktop/24px mobile, 2-col gutter 24px desktop, stacked cols 24px vertical gap mobile, card padding 16px, image-text gap 16px.
No margin between stacked sections; adjacent sections use own padding; sections are edge-to-edge for flush backgrounds and wave shapes.
Use a 12-column grid within 552px content area (600 - 2×24), with half-width columns at 264px + 24px gutter.
Use white-outlined, transparent-fill rounded-rectangle callout (callout.strategic_question.border) exclusively for key strategic questions.
Use white banner callout at 80% opacity over background for 'IBSRELA WORKS DIFFERENTLY…' claim.
Scene-paired banner callouts on white use fixed colors: Periwinkle 80% Office, Purple 60% Cafe, Green 40% Soccer; no mixing scene-color pairs.
Use approved icon trio (Constipation, Belly Pain, Bloating) left-to-right, circular green-tinted style, labels below in navy #262262 for IBS-C symptoms.
All three symptom icons must appear together, in order, without isolation, reordering, or alternate styles.
Symptom trio on green/teal panel or white above it, followed by headline "Different matters: Because one size does not fit all," then body copy and CTA.
All text and CTA buttons are left-aligned; CTA buttons align flush with the left edge of the text block above, never centered or right-aligned.
In the CTA section, supporting visuals sit right of text in two columns; without visuals, text is left-aligned full column width.

Parent-brand typography in Avenir: H1 Black Title Case Dark Blue/Purple; H2 Black Sentence case Dark Blue/Purple; Body/ISI body Roman Sentence case Black; ISI subheads Black ALL CAPS; Footnotes Book Sentence case Black; Campaign headline Black ALL CAPS campaign palette; Callouts black ALL CAPS per spec.
Avenir Heavy is exclusive to ISI boxed warning text; not for any other type level.

In email/PowerPoint, substitute Avenir with Arial stack (Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif); no webfont; map weights: Avenir Black/Heavy → Arial Bold, Avenir Roman/Book → Arial Regular or smaller for footnotes.
Editorial typography: H1 24px desktop/22px mobile bold 1.25 LH, H2 16px bold 1.5 LH, body 16px regular 1.5 LH, CTA 14px bold 1.2 LH white pill teal label.
End Matter typography fixed: ISI subheads 14px Bold, 'IMPORTANT RISK INFORMATION' 15px Bold, ISI body 13px Regular, footnotes/abbr 11px Regular, line-height 1.4, not restyled by content defaults.
Letter-spacing is 0 for body text; ALL-CAPS callouts and CTAs use +0.5px; no negative tracking allowed in email.
Paragraph spacing in sections: headline→subhead 8px; subhead→body 12px; headline→body (no subhead) 12px; body paragraphs 12px; body→CTA 16px.
Spacing is section-owned; Editorial: 12x24x12x24px white; Dark feature: 24px navy/purple; Inset card: Editorial +12px radius +24px padding; gutters 24px always.
Spacing rules: Editorial→Editorial 24px; Editorial↔Dark feature 36px; Dark feature→Dark feature 48px; Full-bleed hero/swirl (0)→first/last section 12px.
Never override vertical padding token; no empty spacer rows or placeholder cells, as they add 12–16px and disrupt layout calculations.
End Matter ISI sections and legal footer/unsubscribe block are fixed, not restyled by content defaults.
Headlines in Title Case; subheads and body in sentence case; callouts and CTAs in ALL CAPS per respective case tokens.
Campaign-specific headlines (FLIP THE SCRIPT) use ALL CAPS as specified by campaign.headline.case token.
Capitalize each word of campaign name inside body copy as per campaign.body_name.case (title case), e.g., 'Flip The Script', not all caps or lowercase.
In 'FLIP THE SCRIPT' lockup, 'THE' is upside down; 'ON IBS-C' uses Avenir Light with 50% opacity, hyphen replaced by 100% opacity swoosh; weights/opacities per campaign tokens.
Use Arial Bold for headlines, subheads, card titles, CTA labels, ISI subheads, boxed-warning text, and MLR-approved key claim phrases only.
Avoid bolding entire body paragraphs, footnotes, references, links (underline links instead), or over ~10 consecutive words in body text.
No bold+italic in email body; use Arial Italic only for journal titles in references or when specified by approved copy deck.
Avoid bold for hierarchy; use subheads or approved callout styles to highlight passages instead.
SFMC emails stack components in order: 1) Top Matter LOCKED, 2) optional Primary 1 Col, 3) optional Primary 2 Col, 4) optional repeated Secondary, 5) End Matter LOCKED.
Use one of two wireframe templates; remove unnecessary sections, don’t add new ones; adjust content to fit templates; omit elements within sections but don’t create new element types.
Provide Desktop (600px) and Mobile (375px) layouts for each component; SFMC auto-serves based on device; all content sections QA'd at both breakpoints.
First content section aligns bottom edge of locked Top Matter (white/theme background, optional wave top); last content section aligns top of locked End Matter with wave-edge ISI panel below.
Top Matter is pre-built/locked by audience (DTP: logo no BID + friendly safety; HCP: logo with BID), includes mandatory safety links; no logo repeat in content, audience fixed by header.
End Matter is pre-built/locked with FDA ISI, Ardelyx contact, copyright/trademark/job code, unsubscribe links; body copy 12px/1.4 line height; coordinate footnote numbering; no ISI duplicates.
Body copy in content sections uses ISI body font size 12px and line spacing 1.4, matching locked footer per §1.2.

Email Primary 1 Column: Desktop 600×1138px, Mobile 375×788px; layout: full-width hero image → purple H1 → bold subheadline → body copy → teal 'Get connected' CTA; background color theme; optional top wave borders.

Email primary 2-column layout: desktop 600×1088px, mobile 375×1538px stack; left text, right brand image on purple gradient background; white text, outlined CTA; editable headline, body, CTA, image; optional wave, button style.

Email Secondary layout: Desktop 600x458px two-column card row; Mobile 375x1363px single column; each card has brand image, navy headline, body, teal CTA; toggles for columns, image placement, elements, editable content.
Use second person voice; empathize unresolved symptoms first, then present IBSRELA as a way to resume everyday life, not clinical performance.
Use "IBS-C symptoms" (constipation, belly pain, and/or bloating), not "your condition" or jargon; always list all three symptoms together when mentioning benefits.
Patient-story modules require disclosure under story copy: "Individual results may vary. [Name] is an actual compensated IBSRELA patient discussing her experience.
Efficacy claims require qualifier "*Results may vary. Study results seen in 12- and 26-week trials…" and limitation "These results were observed… but analysis cannot draw conclusions.
DTP CTA labels must be short verb phrases, sentence case, 1–3 words, e.g., "Explore IBSRELA", following dtp.cta.label_convention rules.
Rule (cta): Use a single solid-fill primary CTA style for poll/segmentation question response buttons (e.g., "Yes, I have").
DTP CTAs must avoid HCP library default "Get connected" label, as it's exclusive to HCP scope only.
Per-section CTAs: story sections use "Watch [name]'s story"; product overview uses "Explore IBSRELA" or "Learn more about how IBSRELA is different".
